summary:
The presence of diffuse mottled retina without any signs indicating an ongoing or recent retinal detachment resolves the scenario where such features are due solely to post-resolution effects from prior events leading up to complete reattachment of detached tissue layers within eye structures involved directly during these processes. Thus, among options provided for differential diagnosis excluding one condition based purely upon whether residual ‘salt & pepper’ pattern persists beyond acute phase necessitates considering only those scenarios involving non-resolvable pathologies as potential causes remaining viable explanations fitting criteria outlined initially but now more narrowly defined through additional context regarding temporal aspects associated specifically with healing versus chronicity inherent certain ocular diseases mentioned hereafter.
The correct answer excludes cases related to resolving retinal detaches while including others linked closely enough temporally so they can be ruled out definitively when presented clinically
